channel.title: INfinance
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INfinance, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Rents are finally falling in some parts of the country. Here’s who is actually feeling the relief.
item.description: Rents fell nationally in January, but they’re still at a high level due to a sharp run-up over the last few years.

item.title: Here are 7 charts guaranteed to stress you out about the stock market
item.description: The “Magnificent Seven” are breaking down, defensive consumer staples are on a tear — and then there’s the midterm curse.

item.title: Crypto investors who don’t fill out this new tax form the right way could overpay on their taxes
item.description: Crypto investors can expect to receive a new tax form this year from exchanges such as Coinbase and Robinhood.

item.title: Is the stock market open tomorrow for Presidents Day? Will USPS, FedEx and UPS make deliveries?
item.description: Here’s how trading hours and other services will be affected on the federal holiday, which falls on Monday, Feb. 16, this year.

item.title: ‘Am I crazy?’ My boyfriend’s parents say he doesn’t earn enough to marry me. Who is to blame here?
item.description: The Moneyist: “I have a Valentine’s message for you, but you won’t read it in a Hallmark card.”

item.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
item.description: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

item.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
item.description: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia...

item.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
item.description: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

item.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
item.description: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on ...

item.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
item.description: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.
